attacking_style: Pure sprinter who launches final kick in last 150-200m, excels in chaotic finales without organized leadout. Can attack opportunistically in crosswinds on flat stages (Brugge-De Panne straight attack).
team_tactics: Alpecin-Deceuninck provides elite positioning through technical finales rather than traditional leadout train. Won 4+ sprints without visible leadout, relying on positioning superiority in final kilometer over raw pilot power.
terrain_preference: Flat bunch sprints with technical corners or barriers where positioning trumps pure power. Exposed on uphill gradient finishes. Surprisingly strong in crosswinds and echelons for a pure sprinter, with diesel engine capacity.
vulnerability: Uphill gradient sprints (first Tour defeat 2023 on climb). Positioning errors in photo finishes when leadout is perfect. Can be boxed out in technical finales despite strong team support (Vuelta 2021).
key_patterns: Won 7+ sprint stages across observations, including 5 consecutive across two Tours (2022-2023)
Exceptional in chaotic/disorganized sprints: 4 wins explicitly without proper leadout or after crashes
Superior barrier/corner positioning: multiple wins in technical finales choosing correct line (Burgos, Bayonne)
Unusual stage race depth: top-10 time trial results and crosswind echelon work rare for pure sprinter
Vulnerable to photo finishes despite perfect setup, suggesting final 50m power deficit (Tour 2023 loss)
Alpecin positioning excellence: consistently better flamme rouge placement than Quick-Step, UAE despite weaker trains
form_trajectory: positive: Peak 2023 Tour form with 4 stage wins and green jersey dominance, but single uphill finish defeat reveals gradient limitation that may be exploited by rivals adjusting sprint stage selection
